body.title: adopt_next_ticket delegates to cmd_next --max-difficulty=1
body.result: adopt_next_ticket now calls cmd_next --worker igor --max-difficulty=1 via subprocess. Empty result → early return. 3 tests. Commit 316cd7b1.
body.description:
adopt_next_ticket in worker_foreman.py directly browses all sprint tickets and picks the top-priority one regardless of worker assignment. This means any sprint ticket — test fixtures, non-Igor work, anything with status=sprint — is eligible. Fix: replace the direct queue browse with a call to `cc_queue.py next --max-difficulty=1`. If the result is a ticket ID, proceed with goal_adopt + pe_chain as now. If the result is empty (None), return 'no eligible tickets' without creating a goal.

This is the missing piece from D-target-difficulty-tiers-2026-05-19: that decision added the filtering mechanism to cmd_next but didn't update the caller.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/worker_foreman.py (~line 288 adopt_next_ticket, replace pending-browse block with subprocess call to cc_queue.py next --max-difficulty=1)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; use subprocess/bash call to cc_queue.py (same pattern as pe_claim calling cc_queue.py claim); do not import cc_queue directly to avoid circular dep
**Scope boundary:** IN: adopt_next_ticket internal browse → cmd_next call; None result → early return. OUT: pe_chain internals; goal_adopt shape; weighted_ticket_score (no longer called from this path; cmd_next handles priority sort)
**Test plan:** adopt_next_ticket returns 'no eligible tickets' when queue has sprint tickets with worker=claude only; adopt_next_ticket returns the ticket and runs pe_chain when a worker=igor difficulty=1 sprint ticket exists; adopt_next_ticket does NOT pick up a sprint ticket with target_difficulty=2
